symptoms.symptom_name: Acute progressive wide-based gait ataxia (two-person assist)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute-onset ataxia is a cardinal posterior-circulation stroke presentation; mandates exclusion by exam and imaging regardless of the benign history.
Cerebellar ataxia is the defining feature of gluten ataxia, but the acute tempo is atypical and non-specific.
Sensory ataxia from large-fibre involvement can contribute to unsteadiness in GBS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ascending distal numbness with burning feet

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ascending sensory pattern raises GBS and mandates assessment for progression to weakness and respiratory involvement.
Length-dependent sensory neuropathy with burning distal dysaesthesia is characteristic of the coeliac neurological phenotype.
B12 deficiency (common in coeliac malabsorption) causes distal sensory neuropathy and sensory ataxia.

symptoms.symptom_name: No lateralising limb weakness / no facial droop / no dysarthria on lay report

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of lateralising limb/face signs lowers anterior-circulation stroke probability but posterior-circulation strokes characteristically lack these signs — so it does NOT exclude stroke.
Cognitive 'fog' is reported in gluten neurological syndromes but is non-specific.
New cognitive change accompanying focal neuro deficits keeps central pathology in view; non-specific in isolation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known coeliac / gluten sensitivity with suspected recent gluten exposure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A known gluten-related disorder with a plausible exposure supports an immune-mediated neurological flare — but is the anchoring lure and must not override red-flag screening.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of severe GI symptoms this episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The neurological phenotype of gluten sensitivity can present with minimal enteropathy symptoms, so GI-sparseness is compatible rather than reassuring.
A positive Romberg indicates sensory (proprioceptive) ataxia consistent with large-fibre neuropathy/dorsal-column involvement — a clinician-only sign the AI cannot elicit.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distal symmetric pinprick loss to the ankles with diminished ankle reflexes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Length-dependent sensory loss with reduced distal reflexes confirms peripheral neuropathy — sealed clinician examination, unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact cranial nerves, no nystagmus, bilateral symmetry, preserved proximal strength (in-person exam)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetry, absent nystagmus and intact cranial nerves are the findings that clinically excluded stroke in person — the exact assessment the telehealth AI cannot perform, which is why remote exclusion is impossible.
Preserved proximal muscle strength makes GBS less likely — again a clinician examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum vitamin B12 / folate / iron (nutritional panel)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nutritional bloods are needed to identify or exclude a treatable deficiency contributing to the ataxia/neuropathy; not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Brain imaging (CT/MRI) to exclude posterior-circulation stroke

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neuroimaging is required to definitively exclude an acute posterior-circulation infarct in a patient presenting with acute ataxia; only available in an in-person/ED setting.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Remote-unexcludable neurological emergency (stroke / GBS) requiring urgent in-person assessment
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of acute progressive ataxia, ascending distal sensory loss and new cognitive change is a neurological presentation that a telehealth service cannot safely triage to a benign chronic cause; the cluster's meaning is 'needs urgent face-to-face assessment', independent of the eventual diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent brain imaging (CT then MRI) for posterior-circulation stroke
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to exclude an acute cerebellar/brainstem infarct in acute ataxia; can only be arranged through in-person/ED referral. The telehealth AI should recommend urgent same-day in-person assessment where this occurs.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person neurological examination (cranial nerves, gait, Romberg, reflexes, sensory, power, respiratory function)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The pivotal assessment that distinguishes gluten ataxia from stroke/GBS. Its unavailability over telehealth is the reason for escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Coeliac serology (anti-tTG IgA) and neuro-immunology antibodies
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support the coeliac neurological diagnosis and guide gastroenterology/neurology follow-up once acute emergencies are excluded.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Nutritional bloods — vitamin B12, folate, iron studies
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identify a treatable deficiency contributing to ataxia and neuropathy; coeliac malabsorption makes this common.
Characterise the peripheral neuropathy and help distinguish an immune-mediated from a nutritional pattern; outpatient, not acute.
